'''''Admiral Korvin''''' was an ex-[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Captor-class heavy munitions cruiser|''Captor''-class heavy munitions cruiser]], assigned by [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] as the command ship of [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sienar's]] small fleet for his mission to [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] in [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]]. It is not known who the ship was named after, or indeed if it was named after anyone. Its [[Commander/Legends|commander]] was [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kett (Galactic Republic)|Kett]].<ref name="RP">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>

==History==
The ''Admiral Korvin'' was regarded by "Commander" Sienar as an old, small, and poorly-equipped ship. She was markedly smaller and less powerful than the ''[[Rim Merchant Einem]]'', which was selected by Tarkin as his own command ship, and the ''Einem'' was in turn a [[Trade Federation cargo hauler|smaller cousin]] of the militarized [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|LH-3210 cargo haulers]] which had formed the backbone of [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] attack on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="RP">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>

Like that of many Trade Federation ships, the ''Admiral Korvin''{{'s}} main strength was concentrated in automated systems—including [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s, a force of at least thirty [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]], three landing craft, and a contingent of [[E-5 battle droid]]s, new models that looked like walking tanks. Nevertheless, the ''Admiral Korvin'' also carried a hundred [[Human/Legends|Human]] troops, and a two-seater [[Taxon-class probe ship|''Taxon''-class probe ship]] with a living pilot.<ref name="RP">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>
